title: Intraosseous Insertion (EZ-IO)
content_markdown:
#### Indications

- Patients requiring intravenous access for drug or fluid administration. 

#### Procedure

Figure 1

Figure 2

Figure 3

Figure 4

- Select and locate the appropriate site (see **Figure 1**): 
- Proximal tibia = 2cm medial to the tibial tuberosity.

 If the tibial tuberosity is difficult to palpate, (e.g. paediatrics): 
- Locate distal aspect of patella and then move 2cm distally (see **Figure 1**) then medial to locate flat aspect of the bone.
- Distal tibia = 3cm (1cm for paediatrics) superior to the medial malleolus.

- Firmly attach the appropriate sized needle to the needle driver and remove the protective cap: 
- **Pink:** 15mm for <40kg.
- **Blue:** 25mm or **Yellow:** 45 mm for >40 kg.

- Prime the EZ connect with normal saline or Lignocaine for conscious/semi conscious patients, leaving the syringe attached.

- 5wab the site with an alcohol wipe.

- Stabilise the limb.

- Firmly push needle through the skin at 90\xb0 until the tip of the needle touches the bone. Verify the 5mm mark is still visible (see **Figure 2**) if not, attach a bigger needle.

- Maintaining steady, light pressure at 90\xb0, power the driver on by squeezing the trigger continuously. There will be a loss of resistance as the needle enters the medullary space. 

- Disconnect driver from needle. The needle should stand securely in the bone (see **Figure 2 & 3**). 

- Remove the stylet by turning anticlockwise and dispose in a sharps container.

- Secure self-adhesive stabiliser over insertion site (see **Figure 4**).

- Connect the primed EZ-Connect tubing to the needle hub (see **Figure 4**).

- Confirm placement: 
- Endeavour to aspirate marrow or blood by retracting the syringe plunger prior to first flush (absence of marrow does not necessarily indicate misplacement).
- In the conscious patient use the appropriate volume of Lignocaine for first flush. Repeat as required as per clinical guidelines.
- Pressurised fluids should subsequently flow without difficulty.
- Monitor insertion site and limb for signs of dislodgement, extravasation or swelling that might indicate dislodgement from the medullary space.

#### Contraindications

- Fracture in the targeted bone.
- Previous orthopedic procedure near insertion site.
- Prosthetic limb or joint.
- IO within past 24 hours (targeted bone).
- Infection at the insertion site.
- Inability to locate landmarks or excessive tissue.

#### Considerations

- Extravasation.
- Damage to growth plate of bone.
- Misplacement- Infection.
- Fracture.

#### Specific Notes

- In the conscious/semi conscious patient, the port line should be primed with the approved designated dose of Lignocaine. This is to be flushed with 1.2-1.5mls of normal saline in order to push the Lignocaine into the medullary space. Wait 2 to 3mins for Lignocaine to take effect.

Normal Saline.text: Normal Saline IV/IO: 20mL/kg via two – four IV/IO sites.
Dose: 10ml/kg Volume: 30ml
Dose: 20ml/kg Volume: 100ml
Dose: 20ml/kg Volume: 140ml
Dose: 20ml/kg Volume: 220ml

up to 20ml/kg.
Normal saline 20ml/kg.
10ml/kg normal saline unless in pulmonary oedema.
150mg IV via Springfusor  Made up to 7ml total volume with normal saline (7ml will run over 10 minutes)
5mg/kg IV (to maximum 150mg) via Springfusor  Made up to 7ml total volume with normal saline (7ml will run over 10 minutes)
50mg/kg, to a total of 2g – IV preferred  Make up to 10ml with normal saline
100mg powder in 2ml vial (reconstitute with 2ml normal saline)

40mg through primed IO extension tubing, slowly over 2 minutes  Allow lignocaine to dwell in IO space for 60 seconds, then flush with 5-10ml of normal saline Repeat after 5 minutes if needed: 20mg over 60 seconds (max. 2 x repeats) Total max. dose 80mg
0.5mg/kg (to max. 40mg) through primed IO extension tubing, slowly over 2 minutes  Allow lignocaine to dwell in IO space for 60 seconds, then flush with 5-10ml of normal saline
2.5g IV/IO  Diluted up to 10ml with normal saline
2.5g IV/IO via Springfusor  Made up to 7ml total volume with normal saline (7ml will run over 10 minutes).
2.5g IV/IO over 30-60 seconds Followed by 2.5g IV/IO via Springfusor  Made up to 7ml total volume with normal saline (7ml will run over 10 minutes).

. If dilution of the drug is required, open the normal saline ampoule or insert the needle and syringe into the injection port of a bag of saline. Draw back on the syringe and draw up the required amount of fluid for dilution of the drug (i.e. 9mls of normal saline with 1ml of Morphine Sulphate for a total volume of 10mls).
Cannula (minimum 18G), bung, normal saline flush, tegaderm, ambulance cannula sticker and sharps container.

In the conscious/semi conscious patient, the port line should be primed with the approved designated dose of Lignocaine. This is to be flushed with 1.2-1.5mls of normal saline in order to push the Lignocaine into the medullary space. Wait 2 to 3mins for Lignocaine to take effect.
Always aspirate 5ml from the line and discard prior to first use. Flush the line after each dose with 10ml of normal saline.
Flush the line after each dose with 10ml of normal saline.

##### Type
Isotonic crystalloid solution of 0.9% sodium chloride. 
 Contains 151mMol sodium and 151mMol chloride per litre [no schedule] 

##### Presentations
500 or 1000ml of 0.9% sodium chloride solution

##### Actions

- Short acting plasma volume expander

- Also expands interstitial fluid volume

#### Uses

- Initial replacement of fluid, in volume depleted or dehydrated patients. (Volume depletion may be due to loss of blood, plasma or fluid and electrolytes)

- Maintenance of hydration during prolonged patient contact time

- To keep vein open, as IV route for drugs

#### Adverse Effects
Fluid overload 

#### Pregnancy and Breastfeeding##### Pregnancy
- Safe to use

##### Breastfeeding
- Safe to use

#### Doses##### Adult
- **In general, aim to maintain sBP ≥90mmHg: **rate and volume infused is dependent on patient condition. Give 250-500mL boluses, reassess after each bolus.
- **Haemorrhagic hypovolaemia: **aggressive warm fluids. 
- Max. dose: 20ml/kg

- **Traumatic brain injury: **aim for sBP ≥100mmHg 
- No limit on amount - dependent on condition of patient. 
- **TKVO: **10 drops per minute (30ml/h with standard drip set)##### Paediatric
- **Refer to paediatric reference charts for BP goals: **rate and volume infused is dependent on patient condition. Give weight appropriate boluses, reassess after each bolus.
- **Paediatric cardiac arrest: **20ml/kg- **Haemorrhagic hypovolaemia: **aggressive warm fluids 
- Max dose: 20ml/kg
- **TKVO: **10 drops per minute (30ml/h with standard drip set)

Lignocaine.text: May be administered IM: Reconstitute each 1g vial with 3.5ml lignocaine. Draw up dose into two syringes and administer into two separate injection sites.

40mg through primed IO extension tubing, slowly over 2 minutes  Allow lignocaine to dwell in IO space for 60 seconds, then flush with 5-10ml of normal saline Repeat after 5 minutes if needed: 20mg over 60 seconds (max. 2 x repeats) Total max. dose 80mg
0.5mg/kg (to max. 40mg) through primed IO extension tubing, slowly over 2 minutes  Allow lignocaine to dwell in IO space for 60 seconds, then flush with 5-10ml of normal saline

In the conscious/semi conscious patient, the port line should be primed with the approved designated dose of Lignocaine. This is to be flushed with 1.2-1.5mls of normal saline in order to push the Lignocaine into the medullary space. Wait 2 to 3mins for Lignocaine to take effect.

##### Type

- Local anaesthetic

- Anti-arrhythmic [S4]

- **High-risk medication**

##### Presentations
50mg in 5ml – plastic ampoule

##### Actions

- Local anaesthetic effects

- Suppresses ventricular arrhythmias

- Onset SCI: 1 – 4 mins. Max effect: 5 – 10 minutes.

- Onset IV: 1 – 3 minutes.

#### Uses

- Local anaesthesia prior to invasive procedures

- VT with cardiac output, in patients who cannot have amiodarone

- Flushing of a painful IO in an aware patient

- Solvent for IM ceftriaxone administration

#### Adverse Effects
These effects are extremely unlikely in usual subcutaneous doses, especially if the syringe is continually aspirated. More likely if given IV.

- CNS effects: 
 stimulation followed by depression, drowsiness, agitation, muscle twitching, seizures, coma 

- Cardiac effects: 
 hypotension, bradycardia, heart block, asystole 

#### Contraindications
Known hypersensitivity 

#### Pregnancy and Breastfeeding##### Pregnancy
- Safe to use

##### Breastfeeding
- Safe to use

#### Doses#### Local anaesthesia##### Adult and Paediatric
- **ICP:** Up to 5ml subcutaneously 

#### VT with cardiac output##### Adult and Paediatric
- **ICP:** 1mg/kg – slow IV / IO over 2 minutes 

#### IO in an aware patient##### Adult
- **ICP:** 40mg through primed IO extension tubing, slowly over 2 minutes 
- Allow lignocaine to dwell in IO space for 60 seconds, then flush with 5-10ml of normal saline
- Repeat after 5 minutes if needed: 20mg over 60 seconds (max. 2 x repeats)
- Total max. dose 80mg
- Consider systemic pain relief for patients not responding to IO lignocaine##### Paediatric
- **ICP:** 0.5mg/kg (to max. 40mg) through primed IO extension tubing, slowly over 2 minutes 
- Allow lignocaine to dwell in IO space for 60 seconds, then flush with 5-10ml of normal saline
- Repeat after 5 minutes if needed: half of initial dose over 60 seconds (max. 2 x repeats)
- Consider systemic pain relief for patients not responding to IO lignocaine
#### Solvent for IM Ceftriaxone
- AP and ICP refer to the medicine calculator for ceftriaxone for dose
